← all tutorials

Practical Byzantine Fault Tolerance (PBFT) — interactive PBFT.md pbft.py

A cluster of N = 3f+1 replicas agrees on one log even with f traitors. Step through Pre-Prepare → Prepare → Commit, watch a Byzantine primary get caught equivocating, and see the O(N²) message cost explode next to Raft's O(N). Every number is recomputed live in JS from the identical formulas and simulator as pbft.py.

① The three phases — step through Pre-Prepare → Prepare → Commit

What to do: pick N (4 or 7) and click a phase. The primary (gold) broadcasts a PRE-PREPARE; then everyone votes all-to-all in PREPARE; then everyone votes again in COMMIT. Each replica needs 2f+1 matching votes to advance. Watch the message counter climb — that is the O(N²) cost.
N: phase:
primary replica pre-prepare msg prepare msg commit msg
All replicas reach 2f+1 votes in each round → every replica commits the same value. Messages so far: pre-prepare = N-1, prepare = N·(N-1), commit = N·(N-1).

② Byzantine primary — equivocation detected in Prepare → view change

What to do: step through the attack. The Byzantine primary sends X to replicas 1,2 but Y to replica 3. Prepare votes split → no quorum → DETECTED. Replicas run a view change: primary rotates to replica 1, which re-proposes, and the 3 honest replicas agree.
step:
Byzantine primary honest 'X' proposal 'Y' proposal (different!)
The gold line: after view change, honest replicas [1, 2, 3] all commit 'X' — agreement holds despite 1 traitor. That is the whole point of 3f+1.

③ PBFT vs Raft message cost, and the worked gold point [f=1]

What this proves: PBFT's two all-to-all rounds make it O(N²); Raft's leader-broadcast is O(N); HotStuff keeps 3f+1 safety but is O(N) via aggregated threshold signatures. Slide N and watch PBFT run away. The green check: OK confirms the JS numbers match pbft.py GOLD CHECK exactly.
f tolerated = (N-1)//3 = 1
Headline: tolerating lies instead of silence needs 3f+1 replicas (not 2f+1) and O(N²) messages (not O(N)). Modern BFT (HotStuff) pays only the replica-count tax, not the message-count tax.
check: … JS formulas + simulator match pbft.py GOLD CHECK (pbft_N(1)=4, pbft_quorum(1)=3, messages(4)=27, messages(7)=90, honest-agree after view change).
